description.en: Parameters: pen – a dictionary with some or all of the below listed keys pendict – one or more keyword-arguments with the below listed keys as keywords Return or set the pen’s attributes in a “pen-dictionary” with the following key/value pairs: “shown”: True/False “pendown”: True/False “pencolor”: color-string or color-tuple “fillcolor”: color-string or color-tuple “pensize”: positive number “speed”: number in range 0..10 “resizemode”: “auto” or “user” or “noresize” “stretchfactor”: (positive...

description.en: Return or set the pencolor. Four input formats are allowed: pencolor()Return the current pencolor as color specification string or as a tuple (see example). May be used as input to another color/pencolor/fillcolor/bgcolor call. pencolor(colorstring)Set pencolor to colorstring, which is a Tk color specification string, such as "red", "yellow", or "#33cc8c". pencolor((r, g, b))Set pencolor to the RGB color represented by the tuple of r, g, and b. Each of r, g, and b must be in the range 0..colo...

description.en: Pull the pen down – drawing when moving.

excerpt.es: Pull the pen down – drawing when moving.
excerpt.en: Pull the pen down – drawing when moving.

description.en: Parameters: width – a positive number Set the line thickness to width or return it. If resizemode is set to “auto” and turtleshape is a polygon, that polygon is drawn with the same line thickness. If no argument is given, the current pensize is returned. >>> turtle.pensize() 1 >>> turtle.pensize(10) # from here on lines of width 10 are drawn

description.en: Pull the pen up – no drawing when moving.

excerpt.es: Pull the pen up – no drawing when moving.
excerpt.en: Pull the pen up – no drawing when moving.

description.en: Record the vertices of a polygon drawn in the with turtle.poly(): block. The first and last vertices will be connected. >>> with turtle.poly(): ... turtle.forward(100) ... turtle.right(60) ... turtle.forward(100) Added in version 3.14.

excerpt.es: Record the vertices of a polygon drawn in the with turtle.poly(): block. The first and last vertices will be connected. >>> with turtle.poly(): ... turtle.forward(100) ... turtle.right(60) ... turtle....
excerpt.en: Record the vertices of a polygon drawn in the with turtle.poly(): block. The first and last vertices will be connected. >>> with turtle.poly(): ... turtle.forward(100) ... turtle.right(60) ... turtle....

description.en: Return the turtle’s current location (x,y) (as a Vec2D vector). >>> turtle.pos() (440.00,-0.00)
